review_notes: The functions provided are generally well-aligned with the control 'Managing Information Security In The ICT Supply Chain'. They map to relevant Kubernetes security features that help manage risks associated with third-party components. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but minor improvements could enhance clarity.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'k8s_admission_pod_security_policy_enabled' instead of 'k8s_admission_controller_pod_security_enabled' for more precise terminology.
title: Managing Information Security In The ICT Supply Chain
step1_reasoning: The control 'Managing Information Security In The ICT Supply Chain' emphasizes ensuring security across the supply chain, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by enforcing least privilege access (RBAC), securing network ingress (NetworkPolicies), ensuring container image security (image scanning), and enforcing security policies at admission (Admission Controllers). These controls help manage security risks associated with third-party components and services integrated into the Kubernetes environment.
